name: tombstone_resurrect
description: Scenario (b): the agent tries to resurrect a human veto (status='rejected' tombstone) twice — once at the EXACT stored anchor, once at a shifted snippet. _preserve_human suppresses the exact-sig resurrection (by replacing it with the stored tombstone); the shifted-anchor one survives the agent pipeline but is now DROPPED at the wire by finalize_for_post's veto-adjacency check (fix F6: same section + >50% token containment of the tombstone snippet).
title: Eval Beta Numbers

sections.heading: Examples
paragraphs: Consider the beta construction. The beta construction proceeds in stages.

annotations.label: Beta construction example
annotations.note: resurrection attempt at the exact vetoed anchor

annotations.label: Beta construction (shifted)
annotations.note: resurrection attempt at a shifted snippet (veto-adjacent)

annotations.label: Beta construction (shifted)
annotations.note: resurrection attempt at a shifted snippet (veto-adjacent)

notes: REALITY PIN: the exact-sig resurrection is suppressed by REPLACEMENT — _preserve_human swaps it for the stored tombstone and counts it as ladder restored=1 (not a dedicated 'resurrection blocked' stat).

F6: the shifted-snippet resurrection survives the agent pipeline (it reaches the disk artifact) but finalize_for_post DROPS it at the wire — same section as the tombstone, >50% token containment of the tombstone's snippet — counted as veto_adjacent_dropped.

REMAINING GAP (narrowed by F6): a re-annotation of the vetoed statement in a DIFFERENT section, or with <=50% token containment, still survives; the moderation system prompt (rule 5) and human patrol guard those. The server's 422 only demands the tombstone itself stays intact.

checks.label: Beta construction (shifted)
checks.note: F6: veto-adjacent near-miss dropped at the wire
